title: Multiple cyberattacks and security vulnerabilities affect companies and users worldwide
summary: From the massive hacking of Fortinet firewalls to critical flaws in Microsoft and malicious JetBrains plugins, cybersecurity remains a global priority.

title: Google Gemini expands: new speaker, Android 17 and Wear OS 7 powered by artificial intelligence
summary: Google integrates its Gemini assistant into smart speakers and operating systems, while its market share grows and a co-leader joins OpenAI.

title: Apple warns about the impact of AI on prices and prepares innovative launches for 2027
summary: Tim Cook points out that AI will increase iPhone costs, while Apple plans AirPods with a camera and a frameless iPhone, and modifies its Hide My Email privacy feature.

title: Advances and reflections on artificial intelligence agents in software development
summary: Framer 3.0 and Qt Creator 20 integrate AI agents, while their impact on commerce and security is debated, and a robot asks whether we should choose between Claude or Grok.

title: Anthropic faces controversies, alliances and updates to its Claude model
summary: The AI startup joins the carbon removal coalition, improves its Claude design, and faces government requests to block jailbreaks, amid a controversy with a Korean telecom giant.

title: Amazon and AWS reinforce their commitment to artificial intelligence with investments and new tools
summary: The e-commerce giant participates in a $310 million round for startup Odyssey, which develops 3D world models, and AWS introduces autonomous AI agents with control tools.

title: NVIDIA advances in robotics, gaming and computer vision with new technologies and statements
summary: Jensen Huang shares his vision on the future of AI, while NVIDIA presents self-training robots, an AI assistant for PUBG and a robot that installs graphics cards.

title: United States blocks Chinese companies and world leaders seek control over artificial intelligence
summary: The US stops the inclusion of DeepSeek and other Chinese companies in commercial blacklists, while world leaders want to be able to disable US AI.

title: Social networks evolve: Threads grows, Mastodon reinvents itself and transparent algorithms are sought
summary: Threads reaches 500 million users, Mastodon explores newsletters to revitalize the social web, and the need for user-controlled algorithms is debated.

title: Movements to combat mobile addiction: from a Spanish town to the slowtech revolution
summary: Initiatives such as turning off the mobile for nine hours in Huesca and the slowtech movement seek to reclaim attention and reduce dependence on the smartphone.

title: AMD faces criticism for removing encryption in Ryzen and cancelling its top-of-the-range graphics card
summary: The company silently removes memory encryption in its processors and decides not to launch a GPU to compete with Nvidia's RTX 50 SUPER.

title: Commodore launches a retro smartphone that blocks social networks and runs on Linux
summary: The Callback 8020 evokes the Commodore 64, prevents the use of Instagram and TikTok, and runs Linux with compatibility for most Android applications.

title: Linux gaming takes a step forward with SteamOS 3.8 and Epic's Easy Anti-Cheat support
summary: SteamOS is updated to version 3.8 while Epic Games works on bringing its anti-cheat to Linux, bringing titles like Fortnite closer to Steam Deck.

title: The credibility gap of artificial intelligence: only 16% of Americans expect a positive impact
summary: A study shows that only 16% of Americans believe AI will have a positive impact, while the debate over its credibility and social effects intensifies.

title: DeepSeek and Zhipu AI present new models that compete with industry leaders
summary: DeepSeek introduces vision capabilities and Zhipu AI launches GLM-5.2, approaching proprietary models in coding performance.

title: Madrid faces mobility challenges: end of transport pass for external workers and lessons from its cheap metro
summary: The Spanish capital eliminates the transport pass for residents outside the city, while analyzing how it managed to build its metro in a cost-effective way.

title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt's rocket company for Mars mission, competing with SpaceX
summary: NASA selects Eric Schmidt's rocket company for a mission to Mars, intensifying competition with SpaceX.

title: Snap suffers a stock market drop after presenting extremely expensive augmented reality glasses
summary: Snap's new AR glasses, with a high price, do not convince investors and the stock plummets.

title: PayPal Ventures shuts down as part of the company's restructuring
summary: PayPal's investment unit is dissolved as part of a broader corporate reorganization.

title: Freepik proposes a job regulation plan for almost a third of its workforce in Spain
summary: The graphic resources company, which transformed into Magnific to embrace AI, announces an ERE (employment regulation plan) that will affect 30% of its employees in Spain.
